title: Node <-> schema descriptor-validator parity
precondition: GV-2 built (catalog/builds/GV-2/) — GV-2 -> BR-4 dependency satisfied

subject.method: extracts the JS validator from the CURRENT index.js at runtime (const block + validSha256/validHex/optionalString/validateArtifactDescriptor) -> zero drift, index.js unedited; compares to Python schema-of-record validation; node required; no live worker call

what_it_proves: clean descriptor accepted by BOTH the JS validator and the schema-of-record
6 core minimal mutations (missing field, bad author_id, bad subject, non-hex sha, non-bool schema_valid, empty runtime) rejected by BOTH -> parity on the shared surface
detects the two real divergences where JS is more permissive than the schema
index.js byte-unchanged (extraction is read-only); no live worker /run call

findings_for_founder.divergence: workers/github-app-advisory/index.js validateArtifactDescriptor is MORE PERMISSIVE than verifier/brain-config-artifact-descriptor-schema-v0.1.json: (1) JS accepts artifact_type=brain_worker_bundle (with worker shas) but the schema pins const knowledge_bundle; (2) JS ignores extra fields but the schema is additionalProperties:false. REPORTED, not reconciled — aligning them is a founder call (which is the source of truth).

chess_patches_applied: runs lifted JS against LOCAL fixtures only; never fetches the live worker; index.js unedited
divergences surfaced as findings, not silently reconciled
next_action: TH-5 (offline receipt-replay of the worker pass-eligibility rule)
